mission: Freeze the authoritative Vecells repository topology, package boundary rules, bounded-context ownership map, and import-governance model before scaffold tasks 042-045 create executable apps, services, and packages.

owner_contexts.description: Owns patient-shell residency, route continuity, and calm patient posture without owning any write model.
Owns the staff workspace shell, human checkpoint framing, and bounded assistive sidecar residency.
Owns hub queue, alternatives, acknowledgement, and exception-handling coordination flows.
Owns pharmacy dispatch, provider-choice, and outcome proof handling without leaking into other shells.
Owns support ticket, replay, observe, and assisted-capture control surfaces and package boundaries.
Owns operations-board telemetry, drill-down, and intervention guardrails inside one exact shell family.
Owns governance, release, access, config, and communications review work under one control-plane shell.
Owns submission ingress, promotion, artifact quarantine, and intake safety gates.
Owns claims, secure-link redemption, access grants, acting-scope rules, and authorization policy truth.
Owns booking, waitlist, reservation authority, and external confirmation gates.
Owns message, callback, and controlled-resend contracts while delivery evidence stays explicit.
Owns published observability, trust slices, projection analytics, and assurance-facing telemetry contracts.
Owns tamper-evident evidence, replay witness law, and compliance-oriented disclosure proofs.
Owns runtime publication, release approval freeze, watch tuples, and promotion parity controls.
Shared runtime application plane that stitches shells, contracts, services, and publication tuples without owning domain truth.
Shared integration and simulator class for bounded provider seams, receipts, retries, and non-authoritative local labs.
Only legal shared home for reusable canonical primitives, identifiers, and cross-context invariants.
Versioned publication packages for API, event, and derived representation contracts; never a convenience dump.
Owns design tokens, markers, accessibility vocabulary, and shell-inheritance law.
Owns non-authoritative fixture builders and publication-safe test helpers only.
Owns docs, validation, architectural rule files, and Playwright instrumentation without runtime write authority.
Reserved tools-only namespace for standalone assistive evaluation, replay, or release-control work while live assist remains bounded.

artifacts.notes: Owns hub queue and case-management shell residency; booking, callback, and practice acknowledgements cross the seam only through contracts.
Closes the starter-shape omission for the servicing-site shell and keeps dispatch proof inside package and service seams.
Closes the support-shell omission and records that replay, observe, and assisted capture remain support work rather than operations or governance drift.
Owns operations board and drill-down shell frames while governance handoff stays explicit and reversible.
Closes the governance/admin omission and makes release, access, config, and communications review a first-class shell rather than an operations subpanel.
Only browser-addressable compute boundary; owns route-scoped BFF ingress, auth/session edge adapters, and publication-aware freeze posture.
Canonical mutation ingress and settlement seam; only service allowed to coordinate cross-context writes through published domain entrypoints.
Derived-read and rebuild worker only; no canonical write authority and no browser-owned joins.
Effect worker for dispatch, callback, resend, and provider settlement seams with explicit evidence and idempotency posture.
Non-authoritative local integration lab for unavailable or manual providers; never a substitute for live-provider proof.

artifacts.notes: Only legal shared-kernel home for canonical identifiers, invariants, and cross-context primitives.
Canonical source for published event truth; downstream consumers may not replace it with route-local DTOs.
Published browser and runtime contract surface; shells and services must consume this layer instead of sibling package internals.
Derived representation boundary only; never a canonical truth owner or shortcut around command settlement.
Single legal shared home for tokens, automation markers, accessibility vocabulary, and shell-inheritance law.
Published scope, acting-context, and authorization fences; no app may mint local policy truth.
Shared non-authoritative fixture builders; cannot become a dumping ground for runtime behavior.
Published telemetry and trust-slice vocabulary for shells, services, and release controls; never a shadow write model.
Single package family for publication tuples, approval freezes, watch posture, and runtime parity law.
Canonical package home for the Intake Safety bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Identity Access bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Triage Workspace bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Booking bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Hub Coordination bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Pharmacy bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Communications bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Support bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Operations bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Governance Admin bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Analytics Assurance bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Audit Compliance bounded context.
Canonical package home for the Release Control bounded context.
Reserved docs-only workspace for topology, ADR, and architecture artifacts; documentation is descriptive and machine-checked, not authoritative runtime truth.
Holds deterministic generators and validators only; no runtime mutation authority.
Reserved home for machine-readable dependency and ownership rules that lint and CI can consume directly.
Owns browser instrumentation and parity checks from day one; never a runtime truth owner.

route_families_owned.route_family: Assistive evaluation, replay, monitoring, and release-control surfaces (derived)

artifacts.notes: Conditional reserved namespace for standalone assistive evaluation, replay, monitoring, or release-control work; live assist remains inside `apps/clinical-workspace`.

topology_defects.title: Support, pharmacy, and governance shell families were omitted from the starter shape
topology_defects.summary: The Phase 0 starter shape listed four baseline apps but later blueprints required support, pharmacy, and governance shells too. This manifest closes the gap by freezing all seven baseline apps now.

topology_defects.title: A generic shared-utils escape hatch would have allowed cross-context truth drift
topology_defects.summary: Shared code is now legal only in explicit shared-kernel, shared-contract, policy, design, release, observability, or fixture packages. No generic utils namespace remains in the topology.

topology_defects.title: App shell ownership could have been confused with write-model ownership
topology_defects.summary: Every app row now records shell ownership separately from package and service write ownership. The boundary rules explicitly forbid apps from importing domain package internals or claiming canonical truth.

topology_defects.title: Patient intake and secure-link entry route families are still inventory labels rather than final URL contracts
topology_defects.summary: Ownership is frozen now so later prompts can scaffold safely, but intake web, telephony, and secure-link recovery still need final endpoint publication to replace derived route labels.

topology_defects.title: Standalone assistive work remains conditional until later prompts publish concrete routes and live-control fences
topology_defects.summary: The baseline topology reserves `tools/assistive-control-lab` only as a conditional tools-only namespace. Live assistive usage remains inside `apps/clinical-workspace` until a later prompt explicitly widens scope.
